ORDER RE MOTION BY DEFENDANT AND CROSS COMPLAINANT JAMES MEISENBACH FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER, TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS & DOCUMENTS, FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (GRANTED)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFED ITS RULING PURSUANT TO THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS COMPLAINANT JAMES MEISENBACH'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS AND DOCUMENTS FROM DEFENDANTS, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS - THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: (1) COUNSEL FOR PARTIES WILL MEET AND CONFER ON A SCHEDULE FOR THE DEPOSITIONS OF PLAINTIFF WALKER AND CITY EMPLOYEES SUE GRENCI, TOM CARTE, LAVENA HOLMES, MAGALY FERNANDEZ AND MICKI CALLAHAN. (2) NO LESS THAN FIVE DAYS PRIORT TO THE FIRST DEPOSITION, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO WILL MAKE FULL AND COMPLETE PRODUCTION OF ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT JAMES MESIENBACH'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMETNS SET ONE. SUCH PRODUCTION SHALL BE MADE WITHOUT OBJECTION EXCEPT AS TO ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND, TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY DOCUMENTS ARE WITHELD ON THE BASSIS THEY WILL BE IDENTIFIED WITH SPECIFIC DETAIL TO ALLOW A CHALLEGE TO BE MADE TO THE CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE. JAMES MEISENBACH RESERVES THE RIGHT TO OBJECT TO THE CITY'S CLAIMS OF PRIVILEGE OR PRIVACY AND TO MOVE TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF THE DOCUMENTS WITHHELD BY THE CITY. (3) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00 AGAINST THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, PAYABLE WITHIN 30 DAYS FORM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185. (302/EHG)

Off Calendar Nov-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court modifed its ruling pursuant to the agreement of the parties. The court ruled as follows: CROSS COMPLAINANT JAMES MEISENBACH's Motion For Protective Order To Compel Depositions And Documents From Defendants, And Request For Monetary Sanctions - The motion is granted as follows:
(1) Counsel for parties will meet and confer on a schedule for the depositions of Plaintiff Walker and City Employees Sue Grenci, Tom Carte, Lavena Holmes, Magaly Fernandez and Micki Callahan.
(2) No less than five days priort to the first deposition, Defendant City and County of San Francisco will make full and complete production of all documents responsive to Defendant and Cross-Complainant James Mesienbach's Request for Production of Documetns Set One. Such production shall be made without objection except as to attorney/client privilege and, to the extent that any documents are witheld on the bassis they will be identified with specific detail to allow a challege to be made to the claim of privilege. James Meisenbach reserves the right to object to the City's claims of privilege or privacy and to move to compel production of the documents withheld by the City.
(3) The Court will impose monetary sanctions in the amount of $500.00 against the City and County of San Francisco, payable within 30 days form the date of this Order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Defendant James Meisenbach'S Hearing On Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Lisa Walker
Off Calendar Dec-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant James Meisenbach'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Lisa Walker will be heard at 9:00 am on the Discovery calendar. Judge: Marla Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Deft'S Entire Cross-Complaint As Slapp Suit
Off Calendar Dec-03-2012 Continued To Jan-30-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Defendant James Meisenbach'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Lisa Walker
Off Calendar Dec-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL DE PAN OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 921-2800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. TYLER M. PAETKAU OF HARTNETT, SMITH & PAETKAU, TEL. NO. (650) 260-1066 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The motion to compel Lisa Walker's deposition and the request for monetary sanctions are DENIED. Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16, subdivision (g) imposes a stay on discovery when a special motion to strike is filed. The Court can lift that stay and allow specified discovery to proceed as to the motion, but only if good cause exists. However, the overarching policy of the statute is to prevent a party from imposing ruinous costs on a defendant in an action subject to a special motion to strike. (Paterno v. Superior Court (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1348-49; Sipple v. Foundation for Nat. Progress (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 226, 247.) Accordingly, good cause for discovery will not be found to exist if the challenged pleading is legally deficient and there is no probability that the party opposing the special motion to strike can prevail. (Garment Workers Center v. Superior Court (2004) 117 Cal.App.4th 1156, 1162.)
Taking the allegations of the Cross-Complaint at face value, it does not appear that Meisenbach has stated a legally tenable claim. First, as to the defamation claim, the allegedly defamatory statements that are set forth specifically in the Cross-Complaint were allegedly made in 2010. However, the Cross-Complaint was not filed until October 5, 2012. If these facts are true, that cause of action would appear to be time-barred. (340, subd. (c).) Second, the facts in Meisenbach's Cross-Complaint, taken as true, indicate that Walker's alleged statements were made in the course of Walker's resort to administrative action with her public employer through its officials and as a public employee. If those facts are true, Walker's statements would be absolutely privileged and she would be immune from liability under any theory. (Civ. Code, 47, subd. (b); Gov't Code, 821.6; Hansen v. Dep't. of Corr. & Rehab. (2008) 171 Cal.App.4th 1537, 1546.) Given these points, there is no need to analyze whether Meisenbach could obtain information through other means. Because Meisenbach does not appear to have stated a legally tenable claim in his Cross-Complaint, there is no good cause to lift the stay of 425.16, subd. (g). Defendant's motion for monetary sanctions is denied accordingly.
This Order is not a final adjudication of any issue regarding the application, effect, or substantive merit of any statute of limitations or any privilege. Rather, the Court merely deems Defendant's request to lift the stay imposed by 425, subdivision (g) as lacking good cause, for the reasons stated herein. Court to prepare order. Pro Tem Judge: Jim Weixel, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/jpt
